focus: Drill into zero-relevant-at-10 Cranfield failures and test a deterministic coverage rerank candidate.
reason_started: The operator asked to continue with the next problem group after field-sum left zero-relevant-at-10 failures unchanged.
reason_ended: Coverage-rerank improved all core live Cranfield metrics over baseline and field-sum while remaining candidate-only.

failure_group_result.interpretation: Most zero-at-10 failures have some relevant evidence below rank 10, but remaining zero-at-10 cases likely need recall or vocabulary work.

validators_executed.name: npm run validate
validators_executed.evidence: 26 node tests passed; MDE parser passed; traceability validator passed; fixture index check and fixture evaluation passed.

validators_executed.name: live coverage-rerank evaluation
validators_executed.evidence: Live evaluation completed over 225 Cranfield queries: nDCG@10 0.3095.

validators_executed.name: npm run compare:cranfield
validators_executed.evidence: Comparison artifact classified coverage-rerank as candidate_improved_core_metrics.

validators_skipped.name: Cloudflare deploy verification
validators_skipped.reason: No deployment was requested; GEN-012 leaves the public default unchanged.

reusable_lessons: When zero-at-10 failures have relevant documents at ranks 11-50, reranking can be tested before broader recall expansion.
A deterministic rerank can improve aggregate metrics while still worsening individual queries, so candidate promotion needs transferability and query-delta review.
downstream_impacts: Phase 2 BEIR should test coverage-rerank before considering it an accepted architecture.
Remaining Cranfield zero-at-10 failures are likely vocabulary or recall failures rather than only field weighting issues.
evidence_quality_notes: Live evaluation uses the full 225-query Cranfield qrel set and the configured OpenSearch index.
Candidate remains transferability-pending to avoid Cranfield overfitting.

supports_mission: Improves relevance through measured candidate architecture changes.
Keeps the public baseline stable until transferability evidence exists.
missing: A targeted recall/vocabulary candidate for the nine zero-at-50 cases.
deferred: Promoting coverage-rerank to a released architecture.
Deploying candidate architecture as a public default.
goal_drift_review.mission_alignment: GEN-012 advances evidence-driven search architecture without assuming semantic or LTR components before they earn their place.
